rss_articles.summary: View CSAF Summary Multiple SICAM 8 products are affected by multiple vulnerabilities that could lead to denial of service, namely: - SICAM A8000 Device firmware - CPCI85 for CP-8031/CP-8050 - SICORE for CP-8010/CP-8012 - RTUM85 for CP-8010/CP-8012 - SICAM EGS Device firmware - CPCI85 - SICAM S8000 - SICORE - RTUM85 Siemens has released new versions for the affected products and recommends to update to the latest versions. rss_articles.summary: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-3502 TrueConf Client Download of Code Without Integrity Check Vulnerability This type of vulnerability is a frequent attack vector for malicious cyber actors and poses significant risks to the federal enterprise. rss_articles.title: PX4 Autopilot
rss_articles.summary: View CSAF Summary Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could allow an attacker with access to the MAVLink interface to execute arbitrary shell commands without cryptographic authentication. The following versions of PX4 Autopilot are affected: Autopilot v1.16.0_SITL_latest_stable (CVE-2026-1579) CVSS Vendor Equipment Vulnerabilities v3 9.8 PX4 PX4 Autopilot Missing Authentication for Critical Function Background Critical Infrastructure Sectors: Transportation Systems, Emergency Services

rss_articles.title: vSphere and BRICKSTORM Malware: A Defender's Guide
rss_articles.summary: Written by: Stuart Carrera Introduction Building on recent BRICKSTORM research from Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG), this post explores the evolving threats facing virtualized environments. These operations directly target the VMware vSphere ecosystem, specifically the vCenter Server Appliance (VCSA) and ESXi hypervisors. To help organizations stay ahead of these risks, we will focus on the essential hardening strategies and mitigating controls necessary to secure these critical assets.

rss_articles.title: North Korea-Nexus Threat Actor Compromises Widely Used Axios NPM Package in Supply Chain Attack
rss_articles.summary: Written by: Austin Larsen, Dima Lenz, Adrian Hernandez, Tyler McLellan, Christopher Gardner, Ashley Zaya, Michael Rudden, Mon Liclican Introduction Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) is tracking an active software supply chain attack targeting the popular Node Package Manager (NPM) package "axios." rss_articles.title: When an Attacker Meets a Group of Agents: Navigating Amazon Bedrock's Multi-Agent Applications
rss_articles.summary: Unit 42 research on multi-agent AI systems on Amazon Bedrock reveals new attack surfaces and prompt injection risks. Learn how to secure your AI applications. rss_articles.title: Threat Brief: Widespread Impact of the Axios Supply Chain Attack
rss_articles.summary: Unit 42 discusses the supply chain attack targeting Axios. Learn about the full attack chain, from the dropper to forensic cleanup. The post Threat Brief: Widespread Impact of the Axios Supply Chain Attack appeared first on Unit 42.

rss_articles.title: Weaponizing the Protectors: TeamPCP’s Multi-Stage Supply Chain Attack on Security Infrastructure
rss_articles.summary: TeamPCP continues its string of supply chain attacks, and announces a partnership with Vect ransomware group. The post Weaponizing the Protectors: TeamPCP’s Multi-Stage Supply Chain Attack on Security Infrastructure appeared first on Unit 42.

rss_articles.title: Double Agents: Exposing Security Blind Spots in GCP Vertex AI
rss_articles.summary: Unit 42 uncovers a "double agent" flaw in Google Cloud's Vertex AI, demonstrating how overprivileged AI agents can compromise cloud environments. The post Double Agents: Exposing Security Blind Spots in GCP Vertex AI appeared first on Unit 42.

rss_articles.title: How to Quantify the Business Value of ZeroFox Threat Intelligence (And Why Forrester Did It For You)
rss_articles.summary: Let’s get the uncomfortable truth out of the way first: proving the value of threat intelligence is hard. Not because the value isn’t real, but because the best outcomes in security are the things that didn’t happen. There’s value in the avoided incidents. Takedowns that stopped a phishing campaign before it touched a single customer. […]

rss_articles.title: FTC Submits Annual Budget Request to Congress
rss_articles.summary: The Federal Trade Commission submitted to Congress its Fiscal Year 2027 budget request, in support of the President’s FY 2027 budget for the federal government.View Press Release

rss_articles.title: FTC Publishes New Strategic Plan
rss_articles.summary: The Federal Trade Commission has published its FY 2026-2030 Strategic Plan, which will guide the agency over the next five years.View Press Release

rss_articles.summary: Federal Trade Commission staff warned Tennessee legislators of the potential harm to patients if it passes proposed legislation that would terminate a Certificate of Public Advantage (COPA) that providesView Press Release

rss_articles.title: FTC, Maryland Attorney General Secure Full Refunds and Additional Penalties Against Lindsay Auto Group for Deceptive Pricing Practices and Unwanted Add-ons
rss_articles.summary: More than $75 million in charges may be eligible for refunds The Federal Trade Commission and Maryland Attorney General today announced Lindsay Automotive Group and its executives will return money to resolve allegations that they deceived consumers for years with falsely advertised low prices and unwanted add-ons that led to buyers paying thousands of dollars more for their vehicles.View Press Release

rss_articles.title: FTC Seeks Public Comment on Petition to Modify Northrop Grumman Final Order
rss_articles.summary: The Federal Trade Commission is seeking public comment on a petition filed by Northrop Grumman Corporation requesting that the Commission reopen and set aside a finaView Press Release

rss_articles.title: FTC Endorses Florida Supreme Court Action  Eliminating the ABA’s Bar Admission Monopoly
rss_articles.summary: Federal Trade Commission staff on Tuesday endorsed the Florida Supreme Court’s decision to eliminate the American Bar Association’s (ABA) status as the sole accrediting agency dictating the educational requirements to take the bar exam and become a licensed attorney in Florida.View Press Release

rss_articles.title: FCC proposes $4.5 million fine for voice service provider hosting ‘suspicious’ foreign call traffic
rss_articles.summary: Voxbeam’s actions allegedly led to “financial impersonation robocalls” that were made to American consumers “ using “non-compliant and long dormant accounts,” the FCC said.

rss_articles.title: CISA gives agencies two weeks to patch video conferencing bug exploited by Chinese hackers
rss_articles.summary: A bug in a popular line of video conferencing software is being exploited by hackers, prompting the U.S. government to order all agencies to patch the vulnerability within two weeks.

rss_articles.title: EU cyber agency attributes major data breach to TeamPCP hacking group
rss_articles.summary: The European Union’s cybersecurity agency said the hacking group TeamPCP was behind a massive recent data breach at the European Commission.

rss_articles.title: Massachusetts emergency communications system impacted by cyberattack
rss_articles.summary: An emergency communications system used across a handful of small towns in northern Massachusetts was impacted by a cyberattack.

rss_articles.title: Ukraine warns Russian hackers are revisiting past breaches to prepare new attacks
rss_articles.summary: In a new report, CERT-UA said attackers are revisiting previously breached infrastructure to check whether access is still available, whether exploited vulnerabilities have been patched and whether previously obtained credentials remain valid.

rss_articles.title: Traffic violation scams switch to QR codes in new phishing texts
rss_articles.summary: Scammers are sending fake "Notice of Default" traffic violation text messages impersonating state courts across the U.S., pressuring recipients to scan a QR code that leads to a phishing site demanding a $6.99 payment while stealing personal and financial information. [...]

rss_articles.title: New FortiClient EMS flaw exploited in attacks, emergency patch released
rss_articles.summary: Fortinet has released an emergency weekend security update for a new critical FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS) vulnerability that is actively exploited in attacks. [...]

rss_articles.title: Hackers exploit React2Shell in automated credential theft campaign
rss_articles.summary: Hackers are running a large-scale campaign to steal credentials in an automated way after exploiting React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) in vulnerable Next.js apps. [...]

rss_articles.title: Axios npm hack used fake Teams error fix to hijack maintainer account
rss_articles.summary: The maintainers of the popular Axios HTTP client have published a detailed post-mortem describing how one of its developers was targeted by a social engineering campaign believed to have been conducted by North Korean threat actors. [...]

rss_articles.title: Device code phishing attacks surge 37x as new kits spread online
rss_articles.summary: Device code phishing attacks that abuse the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant flow to hijack accounts have surged more than 37 times this year. [...]

rss_articles.title: LinkedIn secretly scans for 6,000+ Chrome extensions, collects data
rss_articles.summary: A new report dubbed "BrowserGate" warns that Microsoft's LinkedIn is using hidden JavaScript scripts on its website to scan visitors' browsers for installed extensions and collect device data. [...]

rss_articles.title: Hims & Hers warns of data breach after Zendesk support ticket breach
rss_articles.summary: Telehealth giant Hims & Hers Health is warning that it suffered a data breach after support tickets were stolen from a third-party customer service platform. [...]

rss_articles.title: Die Linke German political party confirms data stolen by Qilin ransomware
rss_articles.summary: The Qilin ransomware group has claimed responsibility for an attack against Die Linke ('The Left'), forcing an IT systems outage at the political party, and threatening sensitive data leak. [...]

rss_articles.title: Evolution of Ransomware: Multi-Extortion Ransomware Attacks
rss_articles.summary: Multi-extortion ransomware relies on stolen data to pressure victims with public leaks. Penta Security explains how its D.AMO platform keeps exfiltrated files encrypted and useless to attackers. [...]

rss_articles.title: Microsoft still working to fix Exchange Online mailbox access issues
rss_articles.summary: Microsoft is investigating and working to resolve Exchange Online mailbox access issues that have intermittently affected Outlook mobile and macOS users for weeks. [...]

rss_articles.title: Man admits to locking thousands of Windows devices in extortion plot
rss_articles.summary: A former core infrastructure engineer has pleaded guilty to locking Windows admins out of 254 servers as part of a failed extortion plot targeting his employer, an industrial company headquartered in Somerset County, New Jersey. [...]

rss_articles.title: Microsoft now force upgrades unmanaged Windows 11 24H2 PCs
rss_articles.summary: Starting this week, Microsoft has begun force-upgrading unmanaged devices running Windows 11 24H2 Home and Pro editions to Windows 11 25H2. [...]

rss_articles.title: CERT-EU: European Commission hack exposes data of 30 EU entities
rss_articles.summary: The European Union's Cybersecurity Service (CERT-EU) has attributed the European Commission cloud hack to the TeamPCP threat group, saying the resulting breach exposed the data of at least 29 other Union entities. [...]

rss_articles.title: Claude Code leak used to push infostealer malware on GitHub
rss_articles.summary: Threat actors are exploiting the recent Claude Code source code leak by using fake GitHub repositories to deliver Vidar information-stealing malware. [...]

rss_articles.title: Drift loses $280 million as North Korean hackers seize Security Council powers
rss_articles.summary: The Drift Protocol lost at least $280 million after a threat actor took control of its Security Council administrative powers in a planned, sophisticated operation. [...]

rss_articles.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
rss_articles.summary: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

rss_articles.title: DPRK-Related Campaigns with LNK and GitHub C2
rss_articles.summary: Analysis of DPRK-linked LNK-based attacks using GitHub as covert C2 infrastructure, detailing multi-stage PowerShell execution, persistence mechanisms, and data exfiltration techniques targeting Windows environments.
